items.title: July 13, 1986: Arvydas Sabonis runs the floor for a slam during the Soviet Union's 105-93 win over Greece in the semifinal round of the FIBA World Championship.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
July 13, 1986: Arvydas Sabonis runs the floor for a slam during the Soviet Union's 105-93 win over Greece in the semifinal round of the FIBA World Championship.

Sabonis, who'd recently been selected as the 24th pick in the 1986 NBA Draft (Portland Trail Blazers), scored 17 PTS.

items.title: In his first legitimate game-like action since meniscus surgery, Johni Broome has put up double-doubles in two Sixers Summer League wins.
items.author: Gina Mizell
items.body_excerpt:
In his first legitimate game-like action since meniscus surgery, Johni Broome has put up double-doubles in two Sixers Summer League wins. 

“My first season wasn’t the best. I can see that myself. That’s why I put in a lot of work.”

www.inquirer.com/sixers/johni...

media.title: After an unexpected setback, Johni Broome aims ‘to keep proving myself’ at Summer League
media.description: Broome played in 11 NBA games during a rookie season interrupted by knee surgery. He has recorded a double-double in each of the Sixers’ first two Summer League games in Las Vegas.

items.title: It can be both. Clearly the front office has not been effective.
items.author: Julia Poe
items.body_excerpt:
It can be both. Clearly the front office has not been effective. 

But ownership and the operating chair have committed most of the unforced errors that fans rail the hardest against: hiring TSpoon before hiring a GM, suspending Angel Reese, allowing facility delays to disrupt a whole season.

items.title: NEWS: Imaging Sunday showed that #Magic rookie Izaiyah Nelson suffered a fracture in his left ankle.
items.author: Jason Beede
items.body_excerpt:
NEWS: Imaging Sunday showed that #Magic rookie Izaiyah Nelson suffered a fracture in his left ankle.

Nelson will undergo surgery and is expected to return to basketball activities in 3-4 months.

items.title: Interpersonal tension is interesting, but I think the thing getting missed in all of the discussion around the Diggins situation is the fact that the Sky committed to FOUR injured players (two who are 35+) in free agency.
items.author: Julia Poe
items.body_excerpt:
Interpersonal tension is interesting, but I think the thing getting missed in all of the discussion around the Diggins situation is the fact that the Sky committed to FOUR injured players (two who are 35+) in free agency.

The Rickea injury was a genuine tragedy, but how was this supposed to work?
